status_messages.what_was_done: Collected intent: target='customers', purpose='cluster customers based on their shopping behaviours and pre'
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not validate that the dataset file actually exists.

user_intent.business_purpose: cluster customers based on their shopping behaviours and preferences from the tansactino data

status_messages.what_was_done: Profiled 50,000×23 dataset. Found 11 numeric cols. Claude suggested 6 feature groups.
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not sample sub-populations or run hypothesis tests for distribution differences.
status_messages.doubts: Suggested groups are based on column names; actual feature buildability depends on data quality. Mean skewness=3.6.
issues: Mean feature skewness=3.6 is high. Log-transform recommended before clustering.

transaction_spend_behaviour.description: Captures how much, how often, and how variably a customer spends across all their transactions
transaction_spend_behaviour.rationale: Spend level and variability are core behavioural signals for segmenting customers into value tiers (e.g. high-value vs. occasional low-spend shoppers), directly tied to the business purpose of understanding shopping behaviour

category_preference.description: Captures which merchant categories a customer shops in most frequently and the diversity of categories they purchase across
category_preference.rationale: Category mix reveals lifestyle and preference archetypes (e.g. health-conscious, entertainment-focused, everyday essentials), which is central to behavioural segmentation and targeted marketing

temporal_behaviour.description: Captures when customers tend to shop — time of day, day of week, weekend vs. weekday patterns, and recency/frequency of transactions
temporal_behaviour.rationale: Shopping timing patterns reveal lifestyle signals (e.g. late-night online shoppers vs. weekend in-store shoppers) and recency/frequency metrics support RFM-style segmentation which is a proven framework for customer behaviour clustering

geographic_behaviour.description: Captures how far customers travel from their home location to transact, and whether they tend to shop locally or at distant merchants
geographic_behaviour.rationale: Travel distance to merchant and home city population size distinguish urban convenience shoppers from rural customers who may travel further, capturing geographic shopping patterns relevant to retail strategy

demographic_profile.description: Captures customer demographic attributes including gender and estimated age derived from date of birth, which may correlate with spending archetypes
demographic_profile.rationale: Demographics alone are weak signals but combined with behavioural features they help differentiate segments that may appear similar in spend but differ in composition, improving cluster interpretability for marketing personas

fraud_risk_profile.description: Captures the proportion of a customer's transactions flagged as fraudulent and their overall fraud exposure level
fraud_risk_profile.rationale: Fraud rate per customer is a behavioural and risk signal that can identify a distinct high-risk segment; fraudulent transaction patterns (high amounts, unusual merchants) may form a separable cluster worth isolating from genuine shopping segments

status_messages.what_was_done: Engineered 215 features for 983 customers from 1,296,675 transactions. Builders used: ['category_behavior', 'category_trend', 'category_loyalty', 'overall_spend', 'overall_frequency', 'merchant_diversity', 'geographic_mobility', 'temporal_patterns', 'demographic'].
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not engineer features requiring external data (e.g. credit scores, product catalogue).
status_messages.doubts: Some features may have many zeros for customers with short history. Log-transform recommended before clustering.

status_messages.what_was_done: Scored 215 features (PCA+AE). Removed 142 via VIF gate. Claude selected 40 from 73 survivors.
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not apply p-value gate (sufficient features remain after VIF gate).
status_messages.doubts: 2 high-correlation pairs still present after VIF gate — Claude was asked to avoid both in each pair.

status_messages.what_was_done: Clustered with k=3, ran deepening loop
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Could not resolve oversized cluster C0
status_messages.doubts: Oversized cluster may reflect feature redundancy

claude_decision.reasoning: A single cluster containing 92.4% of customers indicates the current features (gas_transport, home, grocery_pos) lack sufficient discriminative power to separate distinct behavioral personas. Returning to feature selection to engineer or incorporate additional differentiating signals—such as transaction frequency, recency, or cross-category ratios—should produce more balanced cluster distributions.

status_messages.what_was_done: Scored 215 features (PCA+AE). Removed 134 via VIF gate. Claude selected 38 from 81 survivors.
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not apply p-value gate (sufficient features remain after VIF gate).
status_messages.doubts: 6 high-correlation pairs still present after VIF gate — Claude was asked to avoid both in each pair.

status_messages.what_was_done: Clustered with k=3, ran deepening loop
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Could not resolve oversized cluster C0
status_messages.doubts: Oversized cluster may reflect feature redundancy

claude_decision.reasoning: After two iterations, the same dominant cluster persists at 92.4%, indicating that gas_transport, home, and grocery_pos lack the discriminative power needed to separate behavioral personas. Feature selection must introduce transaction frequency, recency, spend ratios, or cross-category diversity signals to create meaningful separation before clustering can proceed.

status_messages.what_was_done: Scored 215 features (PCA+AE). Removed 134 via VIF gate. Claude selected 39 from 81 survivors.
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not apply p-value gate (sufficient features remain after VIF gate).
status_messages.doubts: 6 high-correlation pairs still present after VIF gate — Claude was asked to avoid both in each pair.

status_messages.what_was_done: Clustered with k=3, ran deepening loop
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Could not resolve oversized cluster C0
status_messages.doubts: Oversized cluster may reflect feature redundancy

claude_decision.reasoning: After three iterations, the 92.4% dominant cluster persists with the same gas_transport, home, and grocery_pos features, confirming these spending categories alone are insufficient discriminators. Feature selection must now explicitly engineer transaction frequency, recency, spend ratios, or behavioral diversity metrics to break the cluster imbalance.

status_messages.what_was_done: Scored 215 features (PCA+AE). Removed 134 via VIF gate. Claude selected 39 from 81 survivors.
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not apply p-value gate (sufficient features remain after VIF gate).
status_messages.doubts: 6 high-correlation pairs still present after VIF gate — Claude was asked to avoid both in each pair.

status_messages.what_was_done: Clustered with k=3, ran deepening loop
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Could not resolve oversized cluster C0
status_messages.doubts: Oversized cluster may reflect feature redundancy

claude_decision.reasoning: After three failed iterations with the same 92.4% dominant cluster, gas_transport, home, and grocery_pos are confirmed insufficient discriminators. Feature selection must now explicitly engineer transaction frequency, recency, spend ratios, or cross-category behavioral diversity metrics — and the orchestrator should raise the VIF threshold to 15–18 to allow correlated but behaviorally distinct spend signals to coexist in the feature set.

status_messages.what_was_done: Scored 215 features (PCA+AE). Removed 142 via VIF gate. Claude selected 40 from 73 survivors.
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not apply p-value gate (sufficient features remain after VIF gate).
status_messages.doubts: 2 high-correlation pairs still present after VIF gate — Claude was asked to avoid both in each pair.

status_messages.what_was_done: Used KMeans with k=8 (auto-selected via silhouette). Ran deepening loop → 8 leaf clusters. Silhouette=0.1536 (weak but usable).
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not try DBSCAN or GMM (not in current skill set).
status_messages.doubts: Silhouette improved 0.124→0.154 across k range — marginal gain.
issues: Silhouette=0.154 < 0.25 — clusters may overlap slightly. Consider different k or algorithm.

status_messages.what_was_done: Named 8 clusters using Claude (tone='easy'). Clarity Gate PASSED. Avg confidence=7.2/10.
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not validate that description text references specific numbers (only checked confidence and name uniqueness).

persona_names.3: The Heavy In-Store & Online Shopper with Personal Care Habit
persona_names.4: The Moderate All-Around Spender with Low Online Grocery Use
persona_names.5: The Frequent Driver & Gym-Goer Who Skips Online Grocery
persona_names.7: The Home-Focused Traveller with Big Grocery & Shopping Baskets

status_messages.what_was_done: Trained RF, 5-fold stratified CV. CV macro-F1=0.982 ≥ 0.7 threshold.
status_messages.doubts: Lowest-F1 personas: The Home-Focused Traveller with Big Grocery & Shopping Baskets(0.95), The High-Frequency Everything Spender(0.97), The Heavy In-Store & Online Shopper with Personal Care Habit(0.97)
